ocr.text: The first table presents a standard curve and sample analysis with headers for Human genomic DNA [ng], CTm ± SD, Sample,
CTm ± SD, and ng. The row for 200 ng indicates a CTm of 19.02 ± 0.45 for the Vehicle sample, yielding 121.87 ± 1.22 ng. The
20 ng row shows a CTm of 22.67 ± 0.83 for the CP sample, resulting in 145.91 ± 2.67 ng. The 2 ng row lists a CTm of 26.37 ±
0.52 for the bevaciz sample, with an output of 22.93* ± 0.04 ng.

Baseline chronic steroid use – full cohort
Baseline chronic steroid use – propensity matcheda
The 0.2 ng entry reveals a CTm of 29.75 ± 0.85 for the iVR1 sample, producing 0.24* ± 0.01 ng. Finally, the 0.02 ng row shows
a CTm of 33.99 ± 1.03 with no sample data provided. The second table addresses exposure with headers including Total N,
Event N (%), IR per 1000 py (95% CI), and HR (95% CI) across three models. For baseline chronic steroid use in the full
cohort, the steroid user row states a total of 677, with 132 events (19.5%), an IR of 37.99 (32.03–45.06), and HRs of 2.78
user group totals 29,506 with 2,461 events (8.5%) and a reference value for all models. In the propensity-matched analysis, the
steroid user data shows 538 total with 103 events (19.2%) and respective HRs of 1.89 (1.38–2.60) for Crude, 1.97 (1.43–2.72)
for Model 1, 1.98 (1.44–2.73) for Model 2, and 2.01 (1.45–2.78) for Model 3, while the non-user group shows 538 total with 60
events (11.2%) and reference values.
